definition: SNAP enrollment and farm bill commodity programs create a $130-140B annual spending floor that expands counter-cyclically during consolidation attempts. Farm-state senators (40+ Senate seats) have effective veto power, creating fiscal convexity where consolidation automatically increases agricultural spending. This is a structural obstacle to US fiscal adjustment.
evidence: SNAP enrollment responds counter-cyclically ($5-8B per percentage point of unemployment increase)
Farm bill commodity programs trigger payments when crop prices fall
40+ Senate seats in farm states create political veto power over consolidation
Pair_1 debate: 'an elegant structural argument' with political economy teeth
open_questions: What is the magnitude of SNAP/farm bill fiscal convexity relative to total consolidation targets?
Could farm bill reform decouple SNAP from agricultural programs?
How does biofuel mandate caloric diversion (36% of corn, ~30-32M acres) compound the fiscal trap?
